persona.name: The planner hobbyist
persona.description: Journal and planner enthusiasts who treat their notebooks as a daily ritual and want a few beautiful, carefully chosen sticker packs from real artists — not ten thousand near-identical marketplace results.
persona.frustration: Marketplace searches return thousands of near-identical sheets with misleading mockups, unparseable variant menus, and unknown quality. No curation. The gamble is exhausting.

problem: The sticker marketplace is a flood: endless near-identical results, no curation, mystery quality, and a variant menu you cannot parse before you buy. Marginalia is the curated fix — fewer, better, honest.
positioning: The Curated Drop — small, hand-picked sticker drops from real artists, opposite of the marketplace flood.
hero_line: A few sticker packs worth the page. Not ten thousand you'll scroll past.

catalog.domain: curated sticker packs (physical goods with variants, fictional catalog)

commerce_behavior.cart: multi-item with variants (size, finish, sheet count)
commerce_behavior.checkout: standard checkout — address required for physical shipping
commerce_behavior.fulfillment: physical — state machine: pending → packed → shipped → delivered

rights_provenance.note: Sandbox only — no real printing, shipping, or artist contracts. All catalog items are fictional.
rights_provenance.media: Flat-lay placeholder images only — no real artwork committed

architecture.data_layer: packages/commerce-core (local deterministic engine, no external DB or payment)
architecture.ui_source: docs/atm-415/ui/marginalia — port verbatim, no visual changes

kpi_assumptions.nonclaim: Synthetic cohort only. Numbers do not predict or reflect real sticker buyer behavior.

risks.description: UI port may deviate from provided design
risks.mitigation: impeccable critique gate >= 7/10, zero P0 findings, before qa passes

risks.description: Physical fulfillment simulation is synthetic — no real shipping
risks.mitigation: State machine covers packed→shipped→delivered; synthetic nonclaim recorded

risks.description: Variant complexity (size/finish/count) may confuse the synthetic cohort funnel
risks.mitigation: Cohort treats first add-to-cart as engage; first checkout as convert

nonclaims: does not prove real printing or shipping
does not prove real artist payment
synthetic cohort only — not real sticker buyer behavior
physical fulfillment is simulated — no real logistics

stop_conditions.ENGINEERING_REQUIRED: If variant-cart schema cannot be represented in commerce-core without schema changes — stop and surface
stop_conditions.OWNER_GATE: Any change to frozen contracts/seeds/KPI after baseline; any real shipping or payment integration

bounded_adaptation_candidate.description: Variant clarity on product-detail page — making pack contents/finish more explicit at the top of the page vs buried in the details section
